Job1:1 A man there hath been in the land of Uz -- Job his name -- and that man hath been perfect and upright -- both fearing God
Job1:2 And there are borne to him seven sons and three daughters
Job1:3 and his substance is seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 And his sons have gone and made a banquet -- the house of each [in] his day -- and have sent and called to their three sisters to eat and to drink with them;
Job1:5 and it cometh to pass
Job1:6 And the day is

Job1:15 and Sheba doth fall

Job3:1 After this hath Job opened his mouth
Job3:2 And Job answereth and saith: --
Job3:3 Let the day perish in which I am born
Job3:4 That day -- let it be darkness
Job3:5 Let darkness and death-shade redeem it
Job3:6 That night -- let thick darkness take it
Job3:7 Lo! that night -- let it be gloomy
Job3:8 Let the cursers of day mark it
Job3:9 Let the stars of its twilight be dark
Job3:10 Because it hath not shut the doors Of the womb that was mine! And hide misery from mine eyes.
Job3:11 Why from the womb do I not die? From the belly I have come forth and gasp!
Job3:12 Wherefore have knees been before me? And what [are] breasts
Job3:13 For now
Job3:14 With kings and counsellors of earth
Job3:15 Or with princes -- they have gold
Job3:16 (Or as a hidden abortion I am not
Job3:17 There the wicked have ceased troubling
Job3:18 Together prisoners have been at ease
Job3:19 Small and great [are] there the same. And a servant [is] free from his lord.
Job3:20 Why giveth He to the miserable light
Job3:21 Who are waiting for death
Job3:22 Who are glad -- unto joy
Job3:23 To a man whose way hath been hidden
Job3:24 For before my food
Job3:25 For a fear I feared and it meeteth me
Job3:26 I was not safe -- nor was I quiet -- Nor was I at rest -- and trouble cometh!
Job4:1 And Eliphaz the Temanite answereth and saith: --
Job4:2 Hath one tried a word with thee? -- Thou art weary! And to keep in words who is able?

Job4:4 The stumbling one do thy words raise up
Job4:5 But now
Job4:6 Is not thy reverence thy confidence? Thy hope -- the perfection of thy ways?
Job4:7 Remember

Job4:9 From the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of a lion
Job4:11 An old lion is perishing without prey
Job4:12 And unto me a thing is secretly brought
Job4:13 In thoughts from visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear hath met me
Job4:15 And a spirit before my face doth pass
Job4:16 It standeth

Job5:1 Pray

Job5:3 I -- I have seen the perverse taking root
Job5:4 Far are his sons from safety
Job5:5 Whose harvest the hungry doth eat
Job5:6 For sorrow cometh not forth from the dust
Job5:7 For man to misery is born

Job5:9 Doing great things

Job5:11 To set the low on a high place

Job6:2 O that my provocation were thoroughly weighed
Job6:3 For now
Job6:4 For arrows of the Mighty [are] with me
Job6:5 Brayeth a wild ass over tender grass? Loweth an ox over his provender?
Job6:6 Eaten is an insipid thing without salt? Is there sense in the drivel of dreams?
Job6:7 My soul is refusing to touch! They [are] as my sickening food.
Job6:8 O that my request may come
Job6:9 That God would please -- and bruise me
Job6:10 And yet it is my comfort
Job6:11 What [is] my power that I should hope? And what mine end That I should prolong my life?
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? Is my flesh brazen?
Job6:13 Is not my help with me

Job6:16 That are black because of ice

Job20:14 His food in his bowels is turned

Job20:27 Reveal do the heavens his iniquity

Job20:29 This [is] the portion of a wicked man from God. And an inheritance appointed him by God.
Job21:1 And Job answereth and saith: --
Job21:2 Hear ye diligently my word
Job21:3 Bear with me
Job21:4 I -- to man [is] my complaint? and if [so]
Job21:5 Turn unto me
Job21:6 Yea
Job21:7 Wherefore do the wicked live? They have become old
Job21:8 Their seed is established
Job21:9 Their houses [are] peace without fear
Job21:10 His bullock hath eaten corn
Job21:11 They send forth as a flock their sucklings
Job21:12 They lift [themselves] up at timbrel and harp
Job21:13 They wear out in good their days
Job21:14 And they say to God
Job21:15 What [is] the Mighty One that we serve Him? And what do we profit when we meet with Him?`

Job21:17 How oft is the lamp of the wicked extinguished
Job21:18 They are as straw before wind
Job21:19 God layeth up for his sons his sorrow
Job21:20 His own eyes see his destruction
Job21:21 For what [is] his delight in his house after him
Job21:22 To God doth [one] teach knowledge
Job21:23 This [one] dieth in his perfect strength
Job21:24 His breasts have been full of milk
Job21:25 And this [one] dieth with a bitter soul
Job21:26 Together -- on the dust they lie down
Job21:27 Lo
Job21:28 For ye say
Job21:29 Have ye not asked those passing by the way? And their signs do ye not know?

Job21:32 And he -- to the graves he is brought. And over the heap a watch is kept.
Job21:33 Sweet to him have been the clods of the valley
Job21:34 And how do ye comfort me [with] vanity
Job22:1 And Eliphaz the Temanite answereth and saith: --
Job22:2 To God is a man profitable
Job22:3 Is it a delight to the Mighty One That thou art righteous? is it gain
Job22:4 Because of thy reverence Doth He reason [with] thee? He entereth with thee into judgment:
Job22:5 Is not thy wickedness abundant? And there is no end to thine iniquities.
Job22:6 For thou takest a pledge of thy brother for nought
Job22:7 Thou causest not the weary to drink water
Job22:8 As to the man of arm -- he hath the earth
Job22:9 Widows thou hast sent away empty
Job22:10 Therefore round about thee [are] snares
Job22:11 Or darkness -- thou dost not see
Job22:12 Is not God high [in] heaven? And see the summit of the stars
Job22:13 And thou hast said
Job22:14 Thick clouds [are] a secret place to Him
Job22:15 The path of the age dost thou observe
Job22:16 Who have been cut down unexpectedly
Job22:17 Those saying to God
Job22:18 And he hath filled their houses [with] good: (And the counsel of the wicked Hath been far from me.)

Job22:20 `Surely our substance hath not been cut off

Job22:22 Receive

Job22:27 Thou dost make supplication unto Him
Job22:28 And thou decreest a saying
Job22:29 For they have made low
Job22:30 He delivereth the not innocent
Job23:1 And Job answereth and saith: --
Job23:2 Also -- to-day [is] my complaint bitter
Job23:3 O that I had known -- and I find Him
Job23:4 I arrange before Him the cause
Job23:5 I know the words He doth answer me
Job23:6 In the abundance of power doth He strive with me? No! surely He putteth [it] in me.

Job23:8 Lo
Job23:9 [To] the left in His working -- and I see not

Job23:12 The command of His lips

Job23:14 For He doth complete my portion
Job23:15 Therefore
Job23:16 And God hath made my heart soft
Job23:17 For I have not been cut off before darkness
Job24:1 Wherefore from the Mighty One Times have not been hidden

Job24:9 They take violently away From the breast the orphan
Job24:10 Naked

Job24:12 Because of enmity men do groan

Job27:19 Rich he lieth down

Job28:1 Surely there is for silver a source
Job28:2 Iron from the dust is taken
Job28:3 An end hath he set to darkness

Job28:6 A place of the sapphire [are] its stones

Job28:10 Among rocks
Job28:11 From overflowing floods he hath bound

Job28:13 Man hath not known its arrangement
Job28:14 The deep hath said
Job28:15 Gold is not given for it
Job28:16 It is not valued with pure gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Not equal it do gold and crystal
Job28:18 Corals and pearl are not remembered
Job28:19 Not equal it doth the topaz of Cush
Job28:20 And the wisdom -- whence doth it come? And where [is] this
Job28:21 It hath been hid from the eyes of all living. And from the fowl of the heavens It hath been hidden.

Job31:1 A covenant I made for mine eyes
Job31:2 And what [is] the portion of God from above? And the inheritance of the Mighty from the heights?

Job31:4 Doth not He see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked with vanity
Job31:6 He doth weigh me in righteous balances
Job31:7 If my step doth turn aside from the way
Job31:8 Let me sow -- and another eat
Job31:9 If my heart hath been enticed by woman
Job31:10 Grind to another let my wife
Job31:11 For it [is] a wicked thing
Job31:12 For a fire it [is]

Job38:1 And Jehovah answereth Job out of the whirlwind
Job38:2 Who [is] this -- darkening counsel
Job38:3 Gird
Job38:4 Where wast thou when I founded earth? Declare
Job38:5 Who placed its measures -- if thou knowest? Or who hath stretched out upon it a line?
Job38:6 On what have its sockets been sunk? Or who hath cast its corner-stone?
Job38:7 In the singing together of stars of morning
Job38:8 And He shutteth up with doors the sea
Job38:9 In My making a cloud its clothing
Job38:10 And I measure over it My statute
Job38:11 And say
Job38:12 Hast thou commanded morning since thy days? Causest thou the dawn to know its place?
Job38:13 To take hold on the skirts of the earth
Job38:14 It turneth itself as clay of a seal And they station themselves as clothed.
Job38:15 And withheld from the wicked is their light
Job38:16 Hast thou come in to springs of the sea? And in searching the deep Hast thou walked up and down?
Job38:17 Revealed to thee were the gates of death? And the gates of death-shade dost thou see?
Job38:18 Thou hast understanding
Job38:19 Where [is] this -- the way light dwelleth? And darkness
Job38:20 That thou dost take it unto its boundary
Job38:21 Thou hast known -- for then thou art born And the number of thy days [are] many!
Job38:22 Hast thou come in unto the treasure of snow? Yea
Job38:23 That I have kept back for a time of distress
Job38:24 Where [is] this
Job38:25 Who hath divided for the flood a conduit? And a way for the lightning of the voices?
Job38:26 To cause [it] to rain on a land -- no man
Job38:27 To satisfy a desolate and waste place
Job38:28 Hath the rain a father? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 From whose belly came forth the ice? And the hoar-frost of the heavens
Job38:30 As a stone waters are hidden
Job38:31 Dost thou bind sweet influences of Kimah? Or the attractions of Kesil dost thou open?
Job38:32 Dost thou bring out Mazzaroth in its season? And Aysh for her sons dost thou comfort?
Job38:33 Hast thou known the statutes of heaven? Or dost thou appoint Its dominion in the earth?
Job38:34 Dost thou lift up to the cloud thy voice
Job38:35 Dost thou send out lightnings
Job38:36 Who hath put in the inward parts wisdom? Or who hath given To the covered part understanding?
Job38:37 Who doth number the clouds by wisdom? And the bottles of the heavens
Job38:38 In the hardening of dust into hardness
Job38:39 Dost thou hunt for a lion prey? And the desire of young lions fulfil?
Job38:40 When they bow down in dens -- Abide in a thicket for a covert?
Job38:41 Who doth prepare for a raven his provision
Job39:1 Hast thou known the time of The bearing of the wild goats of the rock? The bringing forth of hinds thou dost mark!
Job39:2 Thou dost number the months they fulfil? And thou hast known the time of their bringing forth!
Job39:3 They bow down
Job39:4 Safe are their young ones
Job39:5 Who hath sent forth the wild ass free? Yea
Job39:6 Whose house I have made the wilderness
Job39:7 He doth laugh at the multitude of a city
Job39:8 The range of mountains [is] his pasture

Job39:11 Dost thou trust in him because great [is] his power? And dost thou leave unto him thy labour?
Job39:12 Dost thou trust in him That he doth bring back thy seed? And [to] thy threshing-floor doth gather [it]?
Job39:13 The wing of the rattling ones exulteth
Job39:14 For she leaveth on the earth her eggs
Job39:15 And she forgetteth that a foot may press it
Job39:16 Her young ones it hath hardened without her
Job39:17 For God hath caused her to forget wisdom
Job39:18 At the time on high she lifteth herself up
Job39:19 Dost thou give to the horse might? Dost thou clothe his neck [with] a mane?
Job39:20 Dost thou cause him to rush as a locust? The majesty of his snorting [is] terrible.

Job39:22 He laugheth at fear
Job39:23 Against him rattle doth quiver
Job39:24 With trembling and rage he swalloweth the ground
Job39:25 Among the trumpets he saith
Job39:26 By thine understanding flieth a hawk? Spreadeth he his wings to the south?
Job39:27 At thy command goeth an eagle up high? Or lifteth he up his nest?
Job39:28 A rock he doth inhabit
Job39:29 From thence he hath sought food
Job39:30 And his brood gulph up blood

Job41:1 Dost thou draw leviathan with an angle? And with a rope thou lettest down -- his tongue?
Job41:2 Dost thou put a reed in his nose? And with a thorn pierce his jaw?
Job41:3 Doth he multiply unto thee supplications? Doth he speak unto thee tender things?
Job41:4 Doth he make a covenant with thee? Dost thou take him for a servant age-during?
Job41:5 Dost thou play with him as a bird? And dost thou bind him for thy damsels?
Job41:6 (Feast upon him do companions
Job41:7 Dost thou fill with barbed irons his skin? And with fish-spears his head?
Job41:8 Place on him thy hand
Job41:9 Lo
Job41:10 None so fierce that he doth awake him
Job41:11 Who hath brought before Me and I repay? Under the whole heavens it [is] mine.
Job41:12 I do not keep silent concerning his parts
Job41:13 Who hath uncovered the face of his clothing? Within his double bridle who doth enter?
Job41:14 The doors of his face who hath opened? Round about his teeth [are] terrible.
Job41:15 A pride -- strong ones of shields
Job41:16 One unto another they draw nigh
Job41:17 One unto another they adhere
Job41:18 His sneezings cause light to shine
Job41:19 Out of his mouth do flames go
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils goeth forth smoke
Job41:21 His breath setteth coals on fire
Job41:22 In his neck lodge doth strength
Job41:23 The flakes of his flesh have adhered -- Firm upon him -- it is not moved.
Job41:24 His heart [is] firm as a stone
Job41:25 From his rising are the mighty afraid
Job41:26 The sword of his overtaker standeth not
Job41:27 He reckoneth iron as straw
Job41:28 The son of the bow doth not cause him to flee
Job41:29 As stubble have darts been reckoned
Job41:30 Under him [are] sharp points of clay
Job41:31 He causeth to boil as a pot the deep
Job41:32 After him he causeth a path to shine
Job41:33 There is not on the earth his like
Job41:34 Every high thing he doth see
Job42:1 And Job answereth Jehovah and saith: --
Job42:2 Thou hast known that [for] all things Thou art able
Job42:3 `Who [is] this
Job42:4 `Hear
Job42:5 By the hearing of the ear I heard Thee
Job42:6 Therefore do I loathe [it]
Job42:7 And it cometh to pass after Jehovah`s speaking these words unto Job
Job42:8 And now
Job42:9 And they go -- Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 And Jehovah hath turned [to] the captivity of Job in his praying for his friends
Job42:11 And come unto him do all his brethren
Job42:12 And Jehovah hath blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning
Job42:13 And he hath seven sons and three daughters;
Job42:14 and he calleth the name of the one Jemima
Job42:15 And there have not been found women fair as the daughters of Job in all the land
Job42:16 And Job liveth after this a hundred and forty years
Job42:17 and Job dieth

Ecclesiastes2:3 I have sought in my heart to draw out with wine my appetite
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made great my works
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made for me gardens and paradises
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made for me pools of water
Ecclesiastes2:7 I got men-servants
Ecclesiastes2:8 I gathered for me also silver and gold
Ecclesiastes2:9 And I became great
Ecclesiastes2:10 And all that mine eyes asked I kept not back from them; I withheld not my heart from any joy
Ecclesiastes2:11 and I have looked on all my works that my hands have done
Ecclesiastes2:12 And I turned to see wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that there is an advantage to wisdom above folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise! -- his eyes [are] in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 and I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 That there is no remembrance to the wise -- with the fool -- to the age
Ecclesiastes2:17 And I have hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 And I have hated all my labour that I labour at under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knoweth whether he is wise or foolish? yet he doth rule over all my labour that I have laboured at
Ecclesiastes2:20 And I turned round to cause my heart to despair concerning all the labour that I laboured at under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For there is a man whose labour [is] in wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what hath been to a man by all his labour
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are sorrows
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing good in a man who eateth
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who eateth and who hasteth out more than I?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to a man who [is] good before Him

Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to bring forth
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to slay

Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to rend
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What advantage hath the doer in that which he is labouring at?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the travail that God hath given to the sons of man to be humbled by it.
Ecclesiastes3:11 The whole He hath made beautiful in its season; also
Ecclesiastes3:12 I have known that there is no good for them except to rejoice and to do good during their life
Ecclesiastes3:13 yea
Ecclesiastes3:14 I have known that all that God doth is to the age
Ecclesiastes3:15 What is that which hath been? already it is
Ecclesiastes3:16 And again

Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart concerning the matter of the sons of man that God might cleanse them

Ecclesiastes3:20 The whole are going unto one place
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knoweth the spirit of the sons of man that is going up on high
Ecclesiastes3:22 And I have seen that there is nothing better than that man rejoice in his works

Ecclesiastes4:2 And I am praising the dead who have already died above the living who are yet alive.

Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall

Ecclesiastes5:7 For

Ecclesiastes5:9 And the abundance of a land is for all. A king for a field is served.

Ecclesiastes5:11 In the multiplying of good have its consumers been multiplied
Ecclesiastes5:12 Sweet [is] the sleep of the labourer whether he eat little or much; and the sufficiency of the wealthy is not suffering him to sleep.

Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came out from the belly of his mother

Ecclesiastes6:11 For there are many things multiplying vanity; what advantage [is] to man?
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knoweth what [is] good for a man in life

Ecclesiastes8:7 For he knoweth not that which shall be
Ecclesiastes8:8 There is no man ruling over the spirit to restrain the spirit

Ecclesiastes8:11 Because sentence hath not been done [on] an evil work speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner is doing evil a hundred [times]

Isaiah1:2 Hear

Isaiah1:4 Ah

Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot -- unto the head
Isaiah1:7 Your land [is] a desolation

Isaiah1:23 Thy princes [are] apostates

Isaiah1:26 And I give back thy judges as at the first

Isaiah1:30 For ye are as an oak whose leaf is fading
Isaiah1:31 And the strong hath been for tow

Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness [is] in her skirts

Lamentations1:12 [Is it] nothing to you

Lamentations2:1 How doth the Lord cloud in His anger the daughter of Zion

Lamentations2:12 To their mothers they say

Lamentations3:1 I [am] the man [who] hath seen affliction By the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 Me He hath led
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me He turneth back
Lamentations3:4 He hath worn out my flesh and my skin. He hath broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He hath built up against me
Lamentations3:6 In dark places He hath caused me to dwell
Lamentations3:7 He hath hedged me about
Lamentations3:8 Also when I call and cry out
Lamentations3:9 He hath hedged my ways with hewn work
Lamentations3:10 A bear lying in wait He [is] to me
Lamentations3:11 My ways He is turning aside
Lamentations3:12 He hath trodden His bow
Lamentations3:13 He hath caused to enter into my reins The sons of His quiver.
Lamentations3:14 I have been a derision to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He hath filled me with bitter things
Lamentations3:16 And He breaketh with gravel my teeth
Lamentations3:17 And Thou castest off from peace my soul
Lamentations3:18 And I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my mourning
Lamentations3:20 Remember well
Lamentations3:21 This I turn to my heart -- therefore I hope.
Lamentations3:22 The kindnesses of Jehovah! For we have not been consumed
Lamentations3:23 New every morning
Lamentations3:24 My portion [is] Jehovah
Lamentations3:25 Good [is] Jehovah to those waiting for Him
Lamentations3:26 Good! when one doth stay and stand still For the salvation of Jehovah.
Lamentations3:27 Good for a man that he beareth a yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 He sitteth alone
Lamentations3:29 He putteth in the dust his mouth
Lamentations3:30 He giveth to his smiter the cheek
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord doth not cast off to the age.
Lamentations3:32 For though He afflicted
Lamentations3:33 For He hath not afflicted with His heart

Lamentations3:35 To turn aside the judgment of a man
Lamentations3:36 To subvert a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who [is] this -- he hath said
Lamentations3:38 From the mouth of the Most High Go not forth the evils and the good.
Lamentations3:39 What -- sigh habitually doth a living man
Lamentations3:40 We search our ways
Lamentations3:41 We lift up our heart on the hands unto God in the heavens.
Lamentations3:42 We -- we have transgressed and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 Thou hast covered Thyself with anger
Lamentations3:44 Thou hast covered Thyself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 Offscouring and refuse Thou dost make us In the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 Opened against us their mouth have all our enemies.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and a snare hath been for us
Lamentations3:48 Rivulets of water go down my eye
Lamentations3:49 Mine eye is poured out
Lamentations3:50 Till Jehovah looketh and seeth from the heavens
Lamentations3:51 My eye affecteth my soul
Lamentations3:52 Hunted me sore as a bird have my enemies without cause.
Lamentations3:53 They have cut off in a pit my life
Lamentations3:54 Flowed have waters over my head
Lamentations3:55 I called Thy name
Lamentations3:56 My voice Thou hast heard
Lamentations3:57 Thou hast drawn near in the day I call Thee
Lamentations3:58 Thou hast pleaded
Lamentations3:59 Thou hast seen
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 Thou hast heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of my withstanders
Lamentations3:63 Their sitting down
Lamentations3:64 Thou returnest to them the deed
Lamentations3:65 Thou givest to them a covered heart
Lamentations3:66 Thou pursuest in anger

John4:1 When therefore the Lord knew that the Pharisees heard that Jesus more disciples doth make and baptize than John
John4:2 (though indeed Jesus himself was not baptizing
John4:3 he left Judea and went away again to Galilee
John4:4 and it was behoving him to go through Samaria.
John4:5 He cometh
John4:6 and there was there a well of Jacob. Jesus therefore having been weary from the journeying
John4:7 there cometh a woman out of Samaria to draw water. Jesus saith to her
John4:8 for his disciples were gone away to the city
John4:9 the Samaritan woman therefore saith to him

John4:11 The woman saith to him
